items.rationale: Believes blockchains can crowdsource idle ML-capable compute to break big tech's compute advantage and 10-100x available training power for AI; recurring conviction across 2023.

citations.quote: By tapping into the long tail of idle, ML-capable compute around the world... Gensyn has the potential to 10-100x the available compute power for machine learning.
AI + crypto = collective AI. Crypto mechanisms can incentivize the crowdsourcing of better data sets, machine learning models, and compute.

items.rationale: Sees a multi-chain future as settled and treats interoperability as critical infrastructure; LayerZero v2's decoupling of security from liveness is a recent high point.
items.age_note: Reaffirmed repeatedly through Dec 2023; most recent and confident view.

citations.quote: There is no longer any question that the future of crypto and web3 is multi-chain.
Critical moment today in the evolution of cross-chain interoperability. @LayerZero_Labs just launched v2

items.rationale: Considers privacy a precondition for mainstream crypto adoption; encryption broadens DeFi, gaming, identity, and AI use cases. Reaffirmed at Aztec Sandbox launch.

citations.quote: It's hard to imagine crypto ever reaching mainstream adoption without privacy.
Encryption & privacy will be essential for blockchains to broaden their utility and gain widespread adoption.

items.rationale: Deeply enthusiastic about ZK as foundational tech; celebrated Lasso/Jolt performance breakthroughs as a 'lookup singularity.' Long-running technical focus area.

citations.quote: Lasso and Jolt are groundbreaking. Lasso is already 10x faster than Halo2's lookup argument... The lookup singularity is here

items.rationale: Values L2s primarily for unlocking permissionless innovation atop Ethereum without fragmenting network effects; excited by NEAR aligning with modular Ethereum stack. Holds nuanced view of modularity tradeoffs.
items.age_note: Tempered by modularity-paradox concern about single points of failure (2023-12-11/16).

citations.quote: The most important benefit of L2s is not performance. It is that it unlocks permissionless innovation on top of Ethereum.
Exciting news from @NEARProtocol today about their increasing alignment with the modular Ethereum stack

items.rationale: His core thesis: crypto's deepest value is enabling larger-scale human cooperation and long-term incentive alignment, not speculation. Most-emphasized theme in late 2023.

citations.quote: crypto is a technology with the potential to unlock ever-greater scale of human cooperation—the only way humans have ever solved any problem that matters.
Underexplored — token lockups, retroactive funding, non-transferable tokens, rewards for verifiable work, on-chain reputation, iterated games

items.rationale: Sees music as web2's most broken category and web3 platforms as fixing the artist-fan-revenue gap; doubled down via Sound.xyz Series A.

citations.quote: Sound is music without the middleman; a web3 music platform that is powering the next generation of artists and their communities

items.rationale: Views bridging crypto with established payments rails (Stripe integrations) as an important step toward overhauling payments infrastructure.

citations.quote: Great step forward toward integrating crypto payments with web2 payments infrastructure. @loopcrypto_xyz + @stripe integration is live

items.rationale: Excited by protocols incentivizing crowdsourced, verifiable knowledge (Golden, Freatic), including his first anon-team investment.

citations.quote: We believe in @freaticteam's vision of a decentralized information market and look forward to helping them make it a reality!

items.rationale: Believes crypto-enabled games unlock player ownership of in-game worlds; backed Halliday for accessible NFT asset payments. Earlier-2022 emphasis, not restated recently.
items.age_note: Stated mid-2022; not reaffirmed in 2023, so weight is lower.

citations.quote: As NFTs become intertwined with modern gaming, millions of players will need accessible pathways to digital ownership.

items.rationale: His sharpest concern: views meaningless speculation as the primary force holding crypto back and explicitly rejects the 'need the casino to bootstrap' argument. Most forceful recent stance.

citations.quote: We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.
Don't know who needs to hear it, but being a degen just isn't cool.

items.rationale: Worried that web2 monopolies and 'technically illiterate bureaucrats' in D.C. are deciding what can be built, stifling innovation.

citations.quote: innovation dies when the decisions about what you CAN and CANNOT build are all made by web2 monopolies or by certain rogue, technically illiterate bureaucrats

items.rationale: Recognizes a 'modularity paradox': decoupling unlocks innovation but rigid interfaces and separate chains (for DA, etc.) can add single points of failure and fragment network effects.

citations.quote: It depends on whether modularity adds redundancy... or adds more single points of failure (as with separate blockchains for DA, etc).

items.rationale: Broader civilizational concern that excessive caution toward new tech is the real existential threat; recurring motif framing his pro-innovation posture.

citations.quote: the real existential threat comes from us stopping to innovate... We need to be taking more risks on new tech, not less.

items.rationale: Strongly bullish on Ethereum post-Merge for security, energy efficiency, and finality, and as the base for L2 permissionless innovation. Implicit, not a price call.
items.age_note: Merge-era praise (2022-09) reinforced by ongoing 2023 L2/modular-Ethereum enthusiasm; he never frames it as a token-price view.

citations.quote: Once it's done, I believe Ethereum will be a far superior blockchain than it was before.

items.rationale: Positive on NEAR for innovating across the stack and increasingly aligning with the modular Ethereum stack; protocol-level praise rather than a token recommendation.

citations.quote: We need innovation at every level of the stack, not just the core protocol and smart contract layers. NEAR is leading the way

items.rationale: Explicitly disdains 'tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff' as emblematic of the casino dynamic he opposes.

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff.

risk_regime.rationale: Yahya is aggressively risk-on toward technological/innovation risk—he frames stagnation and safetyism as the true existential threat and urges taking more risk on new tech. But this is a builder's long-term conviction, not a market-timing/price call, and it is paired with a strongly risk-OFF stance on speculation (leverage, memecoins, airdrop farming), which he sees as harmful. So: bullish on building, hostile to gambling.

notes: Yahya's statements are overwhelmingly thesis- and conviction-driven (cooperation, permissionless innovation, anti-speculation) rather than market/price views, so token-level signals are sparse and inferred. His single most-emphasized late-2023 message is a values stance: crypto should build long-term incentive alignment and human cooperation, NOT a 'degenerate casino.' Where older (2022) views on gaming/NFTs and the Merge appear, they are not restated in 2023 and are weighted as persisted/extrapolated. No conflicts requiring reversal were found; his views are consistent and intensifying over time. Many 'excited' entries derive from a16z investment announcements he authored, which signal personal enthusiasm but conflate fund thesis with individual view.
